title: Freestyle VM
description: Schema for a Freestyle Virtual Machine resource. Covers the VM record returned by GET /v1/vms/{vm_id} and the CreateVmRequest accepted by POST /v1/vms.

CreateVmRequest.description: Request body for creating a Freestyle VM. VMs are Linux microVMs that start in under 600ms and can be forked, snapshotted, suspended, resumed, and exec'd.

spec.description: Declarative specification for the VM: image, repos to clone, integrations (bun, deno, node, python, postgres, ruby, java, etc.), workspaces, tasks, and wait conditions.

persistence.description: Persistence mode for the VM: ephemeral, saved, or persistent.

idleTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds of idle before the VM is automatically paused (memory-snapshotted). Pay nothing while paused; resume on next exec.

domains.description: Custom domain mappings to attach to ports on this VM.

snapshot.description: ID of a snapshot to restore the VM from.

git.description: Git repositories to clone into the VM at startup.

VmSpec.description: Declarative VM specification — image, repos to clone, workspace integrations, tasks, and wait conditions.

integrations.description: Map of integration name to integration config (bun, deno, node, python, ruby, java, postgres, etc.).

waitFor.description: Shell command that must succeed before the VM is reported as ready (e.g. 'curl http://localhost:3000').

type.description: ephemeral: destroyed on stop. saved: snapshot retained but billed when stopped. persistent: kept warm/resumable.

domain.description: Hostname to bind. Use a *.style.dev subdomain or a verified custom domain.

vmPort.description: Port inside the VM to map the domain to.

repo.description: Git URL (Freestyle Git URL or external).

vcpus.description: Number of vCPUs (Hobby: max 8; Pro: max 32).

memoryGiB.description: Memory in GiB (Hobby: max 16; Pro: max 32).

diskGiB.description: Disk in GiB (Hobby: max 32; Pro: max 64).

Vm.description: A running, saved, or persistent VM.
